data.title: Binance Research: 95% of Latin American Crypto Users Plan to Expand Holdings by 2025
data.description: A new Binance Research survey across Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, and Mexico revealed that 95% of Latin American crypto users plan to increase their holdings by 2025. The study highlights growing confidence in digital assets despite market volatility, signaling a bullish sentiment across the region.Latin America Leads Global Crypto Adoption with 116% GrowthAccording to Triple-A’s latest report, Latin America is expected to lead the world in crypto adoption in 2024, with an explosive growth rate of 116%. The region currently boasts 55 million cryptocurrency users, accounting for nearly 10% of the global crypto user base.

data.title: Ethereum Whale Sells PEPE Tokens at Significant Loss
data.description: According to PANews, an Ethereum whale known for high success rates in trading has recently sold 188.1 billion PEPE tokens within the past hour, incurring a loss of $2.63 million. The whale initially purchased the PEPE tokens in December of last year at an average price of $0.00002357. The recent sale was executed at a price of $0.000009593, resulting in a 59.3% loss.

data.title: Bybit Allegedly Acquires 71,755 ETH Through OTC Purchase
data.description: According to PANews, monitoring by Lookonchain suggests that Bybit has potentially acquired 71,755 ETH, valued at approximately $197 million, through an over-the-counter (OTC) transaction.

data.title: Buffett Reaffirms Commitment To Stock Investments In Annual Shareholder Letter
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on February 23, Berkshire Hathaway Chairman and CEO Warren Buffett addressed shareholders in his annual letter, emphasizing the company's continued commitment to stock investments. Despite some commentators viewing Berkshire's substantial cash holdings as unusual, Buffett assured shareholders that the majority of their funds remain invested in stocks, a preference that will not change. Although Berkshire's holdings in publicly traded stocks decreased from $354 billion last year to $272 billion, the value of its unlisted equity holdings has increased and remains significantly larger than its public investment portfolio.

Buffett reassured shareholders that their funds would always be predominantly allocated to stocks, primarily U.S. stocks, many of which have significant international operations. He stated that Berkshire would never prefer holding cash equivalents over owning excellent businesses, whether through full or partial ownership.

The letter also highlighted Berkshire's ongoing increase in yen-denominated borrowings, though not following any fixed pattern. All borrowings are at fixed rates, with no floating-rate debt. Buffett mentioned that he does not predict future foreign exchange rate movements and aims to maintain a roughly neutral position in terms of currency.

Notably, this year's shareholder letter did not mention cryptocurrencies.

data.title: NFT Sales Surge With Increased Buyer Activity
data.description:
According to PANews, recent data from CryptoSlam reveals a significant increase in NFT sales over the past week, reaching $152.9 million, marking a 19.60% rise from the previous week. The number of NFT buyers surged by 64.53% to 335,666, while sellers increased by 49.30% to 237,123. Additionally, the total number of NFT transactions rose by 4.73% to 1,574,998.

The Ethereum network experienced a notable 42.71% jump in NFT sales, totaling $79.1 million, with the number of buyers increasing by 36.09% to 41,619. In contrast, the Bitcoin network saw a 26.59% decline in NFT sales, amounting to $20.6 million, although the number of buyers rose by 40.65% to 23,097.

Significant transactions in the past week included several CryptoPunks sales: CryptoPunks #7685 sold for $166,289 (60 ETH), CryptoPunks #3963 for $139,515 (50 ETH), CryptoPunks #5774 for $122,773 (44 ETH), CryptoPunks #9813 for $121,059 (44.99 ETH), and CryptoPunks #8046 for $120,659 (44.69 ETH).

data.title: BNB Chain: TVL incentive program extended by one month to March 21
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: BNB Chain announced on the X platform that the TVL Incentive Program will be extended for 1 month, and the new deadline is March 21, 2025.

data.title: A whale wallet that has been dormant for more than a year withdrew 107.13 BTC from Binance 3 hours ago
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, 3 hours ago, a whale wallet withdrew 107.13 BTC worth 10.34 million US dollars from Binance after being dormant for 1.3 years. The whale withdrew 66.17 BTC worth 2.41 million US dollars from Binance more than a year ago, and then deposited it back to Binance.

data.title: Bybit Ethereum reserves have recovered to 50% of pre-hacker levels
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bybit exchange was hacked and lost more than $1.4 billion worth of liquid pledged ETH, mETH, and other ERC-20 tokens. However, according to data from CryptoQuant, within two days of the hack, Bybit replenished its ETH reserves to nearly 50% of the pre-hacker level. According to Lookonchain monitoring, Bybit may have purchased a total of 106,498 ETH ($295 million) through OTC in the past 24 hours. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: A whale reduced its position by 188.1 billion PEPE in the past hour, with a loss of 2.63 million US dollars
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to ai_9684xtpa monitoring, "ETH high win rate band brother" reduced his position of 188.1 billion PEPEs that he established three months ago in the past hour, with a loss of 2.63 million US dollars. This part of $PEPE was bought at an average price of 0.00002357 US dollars on 2024.12, and the selling price this time was 0.000009593 US dollars, with a loss of 59.3%.
